heading: Immediate information that allows consumers to identify affordable coverage options
label: 18003 Immediate information that allows consumers to identify affordable coverage options
notes: References in TextThe Social Security Act, referred to in subsec. (a)(2)(B), (C), is , . Amendments2010—Subsec. (a)(1). , which directed insertion of “, or small business in,” after “residents of any”, was executed by making the insertion after “resident of” to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 111–148, § 10102(b)(1)Subsec. (a)(2). , added par. (2) and struck out former par. (2). Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 111–148, § 10102(b)(2)“An Internet website established under paragraph (1) shall, to the extent practicable, provide ways for residents of any State to receive information on at least the following coverage options:“(A) Health insurance coverage offered by health insurance issuers, other than coverage that provides reimbursement only for the treatment or mitigation of—“(i) a single disease or condition; or“(ii) an unreasonably limited set of diseases or conditions (as determined by the Secretary);“(B) Medicaid coverage under title XIX of the Social Security Act.“(C) Coverage under title XXI of the Social Security Act.“(D) A State health benefits high risk pool, to the extent that such high risk pool is offered in such State; and“(E) Coverage under a high risk pool under .”section 18001 of this title

children.heading: Internet portal to affordable coverage options
children.label: a Internet portal to affordable coverage options

children.heading: Immediate establishment
children.label: 1 Immediate establishment
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary, in consultation with the States, shall establish a mechanism, including an Internet website, through which a resident of, or small business in, any State may identify affordable health insurance coverage options in that State.July 1, 2010

children.label: i
children.text: a single disease or condition; or

children.label: ii
children.text: an unreasonably limited set of diseases or conditions (as determined by the Secretary).

children.label: B
children.text: Medicaid coverage under title XIX of the Social Security Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1396

children.label: C
children.text: Coverage under title XXI of the Social Security Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1397aa

children.label: D
children.text: A State health benefits high risk pool, to the extent that such high risk pool is offered in such State; and

children.label: E
children.text: Coverage under a high risk pool under .section 18001 of this title

children.label: F
children.text: Coverage within the small group market for small businesses and their employees, including reinsurance for early retirees under , tax credits available under (as added by section 1421), and other information specifically for small businesses regarding affordable health care options.section 18002 of this titlesection 45R of title 26

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 60 days after , the Secretary shall develop a standardized format to be used for the presentation of information relating to the coverage options described in subsection (a)(2). Such format shall, at a minimum, require the inclusion of information on the percentage of total premium revenue expended on nonclinical costs (as reported under ), eligibility, availability, premium rates, and cost sharing with respect to such coverage options and be consistent with the standards adopted for the uniform explanation of coverage as provided for in .March 23, 2010section 300gg–18(a) of this titlesection 300gg–15 of this title

children.heading: Use of format
children.label: 2 Use of format
children.text: The Secretary shall utilize the format developed under paragraph (1) in compiling information concerning coverage options on the Internet website established under subsection (a).

children.heading: Authority to contract
children.label: c Authority to contract
children.text: The Secretary may carry out this section through contracts entered into with qualified entities.
